dimensions.rationale: Spravato REMS-compliant site reimbursement remains the #1 barrier to pull-through. Commercial payer coverage has improved since launch but remains inconsistent: prior authorization burden averages 7-14 days across major PBMs, and step-therapy requirements (fail 2+ oral ADs) persist at CVS/Caremark and Express Scripts. Medicare Part B coverage for in-office administration is established but J-code reimbursement rates at certified sites create margin pressure, particularly for community psychiatry practices. CMS has not issued an NCD restricting coverage, which is favorable, but local coverage determinations vary by MAC. NICE approval for TRD provides a global HTA anchor, but the restricted recommendation (after 2 oral AD failures) signals that US payers will maintain step edits. Outcomes-based agreements remain in pilot stage with only 2-3 regional plans. The MDD-SI indication is severely under-covered: fewer than 40% of commercial plans have explicit coverage policies for the suicidality indication as of Q1 2026, representing a massive addressable gap.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Accelerate MDD-SI specific coverage dossier submissions to top 10 commercial payers by Q3 2026, leading with ED utilization offset data and 30-day readmission reduction evidence. Simultaneously negotiate site-of-care reimbursement rate increases with Medicare MACs showing below-average ASP margins to prevent certified site attrition. Owner: Market Access. Urgency: Immediate. KPI: MDD-SI explicit coverage policies reaching 60% of commercial lives by end Q4 2026.

dimensions.rationale: TRANSFORM and SUSTAIN datasets are well-established and integrated into medical education. However, three critical evidence gaps persist. First, long-term safety data beyond 4.5 years needs proactive dissemination as competitive narratives around chronic esketamine use intensify — the ongoing SUSTAIN-3 extension study must be prioritized for APA 2026 presentation. Second, the MDD-SI indication remains under-promoted from a medical education standpoint: emergency psychiatry and crisis stabilization KOLs are under-engaged relative to outpatient psychiatrists. Third, real-world evidence publications from the Spravato REMS registry are behind schedule — only 2 of 5 planned RWE manuscripts have been submitted as of April 2026. Compounded IV ketamine clinics continue to erode share in TRD without head-to-head data; the field medical team needs a sharpened scientific narrative on standardized dosing, safety monitoring, and FDA-approved manufacturing quality vs. compounded product variability.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Prioritize submission of remaining 3 REMS registry RWE manuscripts by Q3 2026 and deploy a dedicated MSL squad (minimum 8 FTEs) targeting top 50 emergency psychiatry departments and crisis stabilization units to close the MDD-SI awareness gap. Commission and fast-track a payer-grade indirect treatment comparison of Spravato vs. compounded IV ketamine using REMS registry + claims data. Owner: Medical Affairs. Urgency: Immediate (RWE manuscripts), Near-term (MSL squad deployment). KPI: 3 manuscripts submitted; 80% of top-50 EP departments reached by MSL within 90 days.

dimensions.rationale: Spravato brand awareness among psychiatrists is high (>85% aided awareness per IQVIA syndicated data), but consideration-to-prescribe conversion remains stuck at approximately 35%, driven by two factors: (1) the perceived logistical burden of the REMS 2-hour monitoring requirement, and (2) persistent messaging confusion between the TRD and MDD-SI indications. The MDD-SI indication is commercially under-leveraged — fewer than 20% of Spravato prescriptions are written for MDD-SI despite the significant unmet need and the 2020 approval. Competitive pressure from Auvelity (oral, no REMS, rapid onset claim) is eroding share of voice in the 'next step after SSRI/SNRI failure' conversation. Auvelity's DTC spend has increased 40% YoY, while Spravato DTC has remained flat. The brand team has not sufficiently differentiated the intranasal REMS-monitored experience as a clinical advantage (structured care, adherence visibility) rather than a burden. Patient testimonial content is strong but under-distributed in digital channels frequented by treatment-seeking MDD populations.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Launch a dedicated MDD-SI brand campaign by Q3 2026 targeting both psychiatrists and emergency medicine physicians, repositioning the REMS monitoring requirement as 'structured, supervised rapid relief' rather than logistical overhead. Increase DTC digital spend by 30% with emphasis on programmatic targeting of depression-related search and social media engagement. Develop Auvelity-specific competitive response materials grounded in TRANSFORM/SUSTAIN remission durability data vs. Auvelity's GEMINI trial 1-week onset data. Owner: Marketing. Urgency: Immediate (competitive response), Near-term (MDD-SI campaign). KPI: MDD-SI share of Spravato Rx volume increasing from 20% to 30% by Q1 2027; consideration-to-prescribe conversion improving to 42%.

dimensions.rationale: Certified treatment center network is the single most important commercial infrastructure lever for Spravato, and growth has plateaued. As of Q1 2026, approximately 2,800 certified sites exist in the US — a meaningful number, but geographic coverage analysis reveals persistent deserts in rural and semi-rural markets (>60-minute drive time for ~18% of diagnosed TRD patients). Site activation rates post-certification are concerning: approximately 25% of certified sites have administered fewer than 10 treatments in the trailing 12 months, suggesting certification without operationalization. The specialty field team (psychiatry reps) is appropriately sized but deployment analytics suggest over-indexing on high-volume academic centers at the expense of mid-tier community psychiatry practices that represent the next wave of volume. Hub services (Spravato With Me) are functional but patient navigation to certified sites takes an average of 11 days from HCP referral, which is too slow for the MDD-SI urgency profile.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Implement a tiered site activation program: (a) reactivation campaign targeting the ~700 low-utilization certified sites with dedicated operational support and reimbursement training, (b) new site recruitment focused on geographic desert ZIP codes using claims data to identify high-TRD-prevalence areas without a certified site within 30 miles. Reduce patient navigation time from 11 days to 5 days by deploying a digital site-matching tool within the Spravato With Me hub. Reallocate 20% of field force effort from top-decile academic centers to mid-tier community practices. Owner: Commercial Operations. Urgency: Immediate (low-utilization reactivation), Near-term (geographic expansion). KPI: Active treating sites (>10 treatments/year) increasing from ~2,100 to 2,500 by Q4 2026; patient navigation time ≤5 days.

dimensions.rationale: FDA-approved label for both TRD and MDD-SI is broad and commercially favorable. The REMS program (Spravato REMS) is operationally mature after 7 years post-approval, with established site certification processes, patient enrollment, and post-administration monitoring protocols. No new FDA safety signals or REMS modifications have been required in the past 12 months, which is a positive stability indicator. EMA approval for TRD (not MDD-SI) creates a label divergence that constrains EU messaging. NICE approval with restrictions aligns with current positioning. The primary compliance risk is promotional drift around the MDD-SI indication — the label specifies 'in conjunction with an oral antidepressant' which must be consistently reinforced in all field and brand materials. Secondary risk: compounded ketamine facilities operating without REMS compliance create a regulatory gray zone that could invite FDA scrutiny of the broader ketamine/esketamine landscape, potentially resulting in additional REMS requirements.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Conduct a promotional materials compliance audit specifically for MDD-SI messaging accuracy (conjunction with oral AD requirement) across all field channels by end of Q2 2026. Proactively engage FDA CDER on the compounded ketamine regulatory landscape to protect Spravato's differentiated REMS-compliant positioning and preempt any policy changes that could disrupt certified site operations. Owner: Regulatory. Urgency: Near-term. KPI: Zero FDA warning letters or DDMAC citations; compliance audit completed with all materials updated by June 2026.

dimensions.rationale: The Spravato With Me patient support program provides copay assistance, site location services, and treatment coordination. However, patient dropout between referral and first treatment remains unacceptably high at approximately 35%, driven by: (1) insurance pre-authorization delays averaging 10-14 days, (2) difficulty finding a convenient certified site, and (3) the emotional burden of initiating treatment during a depressive episode or suicidal crisis. For MDD-SI patients specifically, the urgency gap is acute — a 10-14 day PA delay is clinically inappropriate for patients with active suicidal ideation. Copay support is adequate for commercially insured patients ($0-$10 per treatment) but Medicare Part B cost-sharing remains a barrier for the significant elderly TRD population. Patient adherence to the recommended treatment schedule (twice weekly induction, then weekly/biweekly maintenance) drops sharply after month 3, suggesting insufficient maintenance-phase engagement programming.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Create an expedited PA pathway specifically for MDD-SI patients targeting 48-hour PA turnaround through dedicated payer hotlines and pre-populated clinical documentation templates. Deploy a digital adherence engagement program (SMS/app-based) for maintenance-phase patients to reduce the month-3 dropout cliff. Investigate Medicare Part B copay assistance foundations or charitable support options to address elderly TRD patient cost barriers. Owner: Patient Support / Commercial Operations. Urgency: Immediate (MDD-SI expedited pathway), Near-term (adherence program). KPI: Referral-to-first-treatment dropout rate decreasing from 35% to 22%; maintenance phase 6-month persistence increasing by 15 percentage points.

dimensions.rationale: Spravato faces a three-front competitive challenge as of April 2026. (1) Auvelity: oral mechanism, no REMS, rapid onset messaging, aggressive commercial investment. Auvelity has captured approximately 12% of new TRD starts in the past 12 months and is growing. Its simplicity narrative (oral, at-home, no monitoring) directly attacks Spravato's operational complexity. (2) Compounded IV ketamine: unregulated, lower cost per session at many clinics, growing consumer awareness through DTC advertising and social media. An estimated 800+ IV ketamine clinics now operate in the US, many without psychiatric oversight, creating both a patient safety concern and a competitive drain. (3) Pipeline: psilocybin-assisted therapy (Compass Pathways COMP360) has a PDUFA target in late 2026/early 2027, which will dominate media narratives around 'breakthrough depression treatments' and could reposition Spravato as 'last generation.' The compounded ketamine threat is the most commercially damaging in the near term due to price undercutting and the absence of REMS burden, but the psilocybin narrative threat is strategically more dangerous.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Deploy a competitive war room for Auvelity within 30 days: arm field force with head-to-head remission rate comparisons (TRANSFORM MADRS remission vs. GEMINI response data), emphasizing Spravato's durable remission evidence. Develop a medical-led campaign on compounded ketamine safety and quality risks (leveraging FDA's own warning letters to compounders). Pre-position Spravato's mechanism-of-action differentiation vs. psilocybin-assisted therapy by Q4 2026 through advisory boards with top 25 psychopharmacology KOLs. Owner: Marketing (Auvelity response), Medical Affairs (ketamine/psilocybin), Competitive Intelligence. Urgency: Immediate (Auvelity war room), Strategic (psilocybin positioning). KPI: New TRD start share holding at ≥40% through Q4 2026; Auvelity share growth decelerating.

dimensions.rationale: Spravato supply chain is mature and stable. Single-source intranasal device manufacturing has not experienced stockouts or supply disruptions in the past 18 months. REMS-compliant distribution through specialty pharmacy (limited network) is well-established with ASD Healthcare and Cardinal Health Specialty. Cold chain is not required, simplifying logistics. The primary supply/distribution risk is scaling capacity if MDD-SI uptake accelerates or if site network expansion succeeds — current production capacity has approximately 30% headroom based on trailing demand forecasts. A secondary risk is that any single-source manufacturing disruption would have no backup pathway for 6+ months. The REMS-restricted distribution model inherently limits channel complexity, which is a supply chain advantage.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Commission a capacity stress test modeling a 40% demand increase scenario (driven by MDD-SI uptake acceleration and site expansion success) and confirm supply chain can deliver within current lead times. Evaluate dual-source manufacturing feasibility study to mitigate single-source risk, targeting a go/no-go decision by Q1 2027. Owner: Supply Chain / Commercial Operations. Urgency: Near-term (stress test), Strategic (dual-source evaluation). KPI: Zero stockout events; capacity utilization remaining below 85% at peak demand scenarios.
